title: DiRT Rally 2.0

notes: No tweaks needed (didn't even have to adjust the graphics or controls); just ran perfectly straight out of the box. Did a shakedown on my first event, then did a full run on the track (all in cockpit first-person), and then watched the full replay (amazing cameras in the game really show off the geography and your car). The volumetric dust doesn't look all that great during the replays, but maybe I can tweak that.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0

notes:
This was just a quick test before going to work after it downloading over night. The game is pretty much plug and play. Click install, click play and you're in. Only thing that is not working for me so far is Racenet, which means no single player also. It is really unbelievable that Codemasters thinks always online single player is a good ideea.

Anyway, ran an event, ran a timetrial, so far everything seems to work as expected. FPS is in my opinion OK. Considering the updated engine and all was expecting less than original Dirt Rally, but seems quite high. 

Every now and then ther is a FPS spike, but from what I've seen that is also present on Windows version.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design
notes: Works out of the box except RaceNET. The quick fix is to append the codemasters certificate in to ca-certificates. To do that, first download the certificate in PEM format from https://www.upload.ee/files/9626680/codemasters.pem.html. Then convert it to CRT format by issuing the command "openssl x509 -in codemasters.pem -inform PEM -out codemasters.crt. Finally append the certificate to the ca-certificate.crt file with the command "cat codemasters.crt >> /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificate.crt". In High Quality, I get around 60 fps but when I switch to Ultra, UI scaling gets messed up and predictably average FPS goes down to low 30s. Other than that it runs flawlessly.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0

title: DiRT Rally 2.0

notes: I had to merge certificate as explained in other reports, after that it works perfectly fine. My gpu is actually an R9 280X, the game  runs at about 60fps in 2560x1440 on medium settings.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0

notes:
NOTE: Racenet will not work without importing Codemasters' certificate. Download certificate here: https://www.upload.ee/files/9626680/codemasters.pem.html. Convert to CRT with "openssl x509 -in codemasters.pem -inform PEM -out codemasters.crt", add certificate to system with "cat codemasters.crt >> /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt", followed by "update-ca-trust". 

Beyond the certificate issue, game boots and runs exactly like under Windows. Performance is great, no graphical glitches or unusual behaviour 3 hours in. Tested with DualShock 4 and Xbox One controllers. Rumble and all other features work as expected, apart from some issues with the DS4 controller, which can easily be fixed by having Steam take control over its emulation via Settings > Controllers > General controller settings > Playstation configuration support.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0

Haven't tried running the game in non-VR.

title: DiRT Rally 2.0

notes:
To get the codemasters certificate working on Arch/Arch derivatives I did the following.
Follow the guide in the comments below. Download the Certificate, convert it to .crt but to install it don't use cat, use the following guide on the arch wiki.
run trust anchor --store codemasters.crt to install

(https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/User:Grawity/Adding_a_trusted_CA_certificate)

And you're golden.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0

notes: Racenet works after adding CA certificate, as with other reports. G29 Wheel properly identified in this proton version,  displaying correct button symbols (recognised as a generic wheel under 4.2). I managed to get force feedback working by disconnecting then reconnecting my wheel while the game is running (needs to be done every time running the game). Soft lock does not work correctly for me: I get full 900 degrees rotation in every vehicle unless lowering steering saturation.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0

notes: Runs Perfect, but you still need to add the certificate manually. Performance is great as well.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0

notes:
Fixed missing certificate to log into Racenet. This is covered by others.

-- FFB on my G29 was not working, so I instrumented the code and have created a working patch for the issue. See: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2366

-- The Linux Logitech driver lacks support for some of the requested FFB effects, but it is still very playable.

-- I normally play with a 1080@60 Hz projector on a 106" screen. At this resolution, the game usually does not drop frames with the "Ultra" preset. There are a few areas where things slow down, so I have backed off the settings on things like "crowds."

-- I have disabled TAA, as this makes the game look blurry.

-- With an libxrandr hack (mine, unpublished), I can bond three  32" 2560x1440 curved displays into a single virtual 7680x1440 display. DiRT 2.0 correctly detects this ulta-wide configuration and renders without distortion. This provides for a much wider field of view, but graphics settings need to be tuned back to keep the frame rate up.
title: DiRT Rally 2.0
